Recruitment Experiences and Outlook Survey

Fuel Shock Monitoring

Jobs and Skills Australia surveys around 1,000 employers each month through the Recruitment Experiences and Outlook Survey (REOS) to understand employers’ recruitment experiences and staffing expectations. From 1 April 2026, the survey introduced new targeted questions to measure the impact of fuel costs and supply disruptions on businesses.

The REOS survey is generally completed by a manager or the person responsible for recruitment. Accordingly, responses relating to costs and fuel impacts should be interpreted as reflecting the respondent’s individual perspective.

Results are weighted and flagged where the number of contributing records is small. Figures based on 50 to 124 contributing records are marked with an asterisk (*), indicating the results should be interpreted with caution, while those based on fewer than 50 records are marked with two asterisks (**), indicating they are not publishable. For more information, see REOS methodology paper.

Disclaimer

Data in this dashboard are derived from Jobs and Skills Australia’s Recruitment Experiences and Outlook Survey (REOS). Estimates are based on weighted survey responses and are subject to sampling variability, particularly where sample sizes are small or results are disaggregated. Results should be interpreted as indicative of employer sentiment and experiences at the time of collection, rather than precise measures.

Fuel cost and supply disruption questions were introduced from 1 April 2026, and findings for these measures are not directly comparable with earlier periods.

This dashboard is for internal analytical purposes only. The underlying data and outputs are subject to change as new information becomes available.
